speaker: MALCOLM
text: Let us seek out some desolate shade and there

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Weep our sad bosoms empty.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Let us rather

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Hold fast the mortal sword and, like good men,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Bestride our downfall’n birthdom. Each new morn

speaker: MACDUFF
text: New widows howl, new orphans cry, new sorrows

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Strike heaven on the face, that it resounds

speaker: MACDUFF
text: As if it felt with Scotland, and yelled out

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Like syllable of dolor.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: What I believe, I’ll wail;

speaker: MALCOLM
text: What know, believe; and what I can redress,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: As I shall find the time to friend, I will.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: What you have spoke, it may be so, perchance.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: This tyrant, whose sole name blisters our tongues,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Was once thought honest. You have loved him well.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: He hath not touched you yet. I am young, but something

speaker: MALCOLM
text: You may deserve of him through me, and wisdom

speaker: MALCOLM
text: To offer up a weak, poor, innocent lamb

speaker: MALCOLM
text: T’ appease an angry god.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I am not treacherous.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: But Macbeth is.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: A good and virtuous nature may recoil

speaker: MALCOLM
text: In an imperial charge. But I shall crave your pardon.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That which you are, my thoughts cannot transpose.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Angels are bright still, though the brightest fell.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Though all things foul would wear the brows of grace,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Yet grace must still look so.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I have lost my hopes.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Perchance even there where I did find my doubts.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Why in that rawness left you wife and child,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Those precious motives, those strong knots of love,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Without leave-taking? I pray you,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Let not my jealousies be your dishonors,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: But mine own safeties. You may be rightly just,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Whatever I shall think.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Bleed, bleed, poor country!

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Great tyranny, lay thou thy basis sure,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: For goodness dare not check thee. Wear thou thy wrongs;

speaker: MACDUFF
text: The title is affeered.—Fare thee well, lord.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I would not be the villain that thou think’st

speaker: MACDUFF
text: For the whole space that’s in the tyrant’s grasp,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And the rich East to boot.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Be not offended.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I speak not as in absolute fear of you.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I think our country sinks beneath the yoke.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: It weeps, it bleeds, and each new day a gash

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Is added to her wounds. I think withal

speaker: MALCOLM
text: There would be hands uplifted in my right;

speaker: MALCOLM
text: And here from gracious England have I offer

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Of goodly thousands. But, for all this,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: When I shall tread upon the tyrant’s head

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Or wear it on my sword, yet my poor country

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Shall have more vices than it had before,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: More suffer, and more sundry ways than ever,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: By him that shall succeed.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: What should he be?

speaker: MALCOLM
text: It is myself I mean, in whom I know

speaker: MALCOLM
text: All the particulars of vice so grafted

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That, when they shall be opened, black Macbeth

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Will seem as pure as snow, and the poor state

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Esteem him as a lamb, being compared

speaker: MALCOLM
text: With my confineless harms.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Not in the legions

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Of horrid hell can come a devil more damned

speaker: MACDUFF
text: In evils to top Macbeth.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I grant him bloody,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Luxurious, avaricious, false, deceitful,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Sudden, malicious, smacking of every sin

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That has a name. But there’s no bottom, none,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: In my voluptuousness. Your wives, your daughters,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Your matrons, and your maids could not fill up

speaker: MALCOLM
text: The cistern of my lust, and my desire

speaker: MALCOLM
text: All continent impediments would o’erbear

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That did oppose my will. Better Macbeth

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Than such an one to reign.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Boundless intemperance

speaker: MACDUFF
text: In nature is a tyranny. It hath been

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Th’ untimely emptying of the happy throne

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And fall of many kings. But fear not yet

speaker: MACDUFF
text: To take upon you what is yours. You may

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Convey your pleasures in a spacious plenty

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And yet seem cold—the time you may so hoodwink.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: We have willing dames enough. There cannot be

speaker: MACDUFF
text: That vulture in you to devour so many

speaker: MACDUFF
text: As will to greatness dedicate themselves,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Finding it so inclined.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: With this there grows

speaker: MALCOLM
text: In my most ill-composed affection such

speaker: MALCOLM
text: A stanchless avarice that, were I king,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I should cut off the nobles for their lands,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Desire his jewels, and this other’s house;

speaker: MALCOLM
text: And my more-having would be as a sauce

speaker: MALCOLM
text: To make me hunger more, that I should forge

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Quarrels unjust against the good and loyal,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Destroying them for wealth.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: This avarice

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Sticks deeper, grows with more pernicious root

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Than summer-seeming lust, and it hath been

speaker: MACDUFF
text: The sword of our slain kings. Yet do not fear.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Scotland hath foisons to fill up your will

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Of your mere own. All these are portable,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: With other graces weighed.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: But I have none. The king-becoming graces,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: As justice, verity, temp’rance, stableness,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Bounty, perseverance, mercy, lowliness,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Devotion, patience, courage, fortitude,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I have no relish of them but abound

speaker: MALCOLM
text: In the division of each several crime,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Acting it many ways. Nay, had I power, I should

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Pour the sweet milk of concord into hell,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Uproar the universal peace, confound

speaker: MALCOLM
text: All unity on earth.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: O Scotland, Scotland!

speaker: MALCOLM
text: If such a one be fit to govern, speak.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I am as I have spoken.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Fit to govern?

speaker: MACDUFF
text: No, not to live.—O nation miserable,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: With an untitled tyrant bloody-sceptered,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: When shalt thou see thy wholesome days again,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Since that the truest issue of thy throne

speaker: MACDUFF
text: By his own interdiction stands accursed

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And does blaspheme his breed?—Thy royal father

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Was a most sainted king. The queen that bore thee,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Oft’ner upon her knees than on her feet,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Died every day she lived. Fare thee well.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: These evils thou repeat’st upon thyself

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Hath banished me from Scotland.—O my breast,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Thy hope ends here!

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Macduff, this noble passion,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Child of integrity, hath from my soul

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Wiped the black scruples, reconciled my thoughts

speaker: MALCOLM
text: To thy good truth and honor. Devilish Macbeth

speaker: MALCOLM
text: By many of these trains hath sought to win me

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Into his power, and modest wisdom plucks me

speaker: MALCOLM
text: From overcredulous haste. But God above

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Deal between thee and me, for even now

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I put myself to thy direction and

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Unspeak mine own detraction, here abjure

speaker: MALCOLM
text: The taints and blames I laid upon myself

speaker: MALCOLM
text: For strangers to my nature. I am yet

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Unknown to woman, never was forsworn,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Scarcely have coveted what was mine own,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: At no time broke my faith, would not betray

speaker: MALCOLM
text: The devil to his fellow, and delight

speaker: MALCOLM
text: No less in truth than life. My first false speaking

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Was this upon myself. What I am truly

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Is thine and my poor country’s to command—

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Whither indeed, before thy here-approach,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Old Siward with ten thousand warlike men,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Already at a point, was setting forth.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Now we’ll together, and the chance of goodness

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Be like our warranted quarrel. Why are you silent?

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Such welcome and unwelcome things at once

speaker: MACDUFF
text: ’Tis hard to reconcile.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Well, more anon.—

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Comes the King forth, I pray you?

speaker: DOCTOR
text: Ay, sir. There are a crew of wretched souls

speaker: DOCTOR
text: That stay his cure. Their malady convinces

speaker: DOCTOR
text: The great assay of art, but at his touch

speaker: DOCTOR
text: (Such sanctity hath heaven given his hand)

speaker: DOCTOR
text: They presently amend.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I thank you, doctor.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: What’s the disease he means?

speaker: MALCOLM
text: ’Tis called the evil:

speaker: MALCOLM
text: A most miraculous work in this good king,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Which often since my here-remain in England

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I have seen him do. How he solicits heaven

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Himself best knows, but strangely visited people

speaker: MALCOLM
text: All swoll’n and ulcerous, pitiful to the eye,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: The mere despair of surgery, he cures,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Hanging a golden stamp about their necks,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Put on with holy prayers; and, ’tis spoken,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: To the succeeding royalty he leaves

speaker: MALCOLM
text: The healing benediction. With this strange virtue,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: He hath a heavenly gift of prophecy,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: And sundry blessings hang about his throne

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That speak him full of grace.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: See who comes here.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: My countryman, but yet I know him not.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: My ever-gentle cousin, welcome hither.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I know him now.—Good God betimes remove

speaker: MALCOLM
text: The means that makes us strangers!

speaker: ROSS
text: Sir, amen.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Stands Scotland where it did?

speaker: ROSS
text: Alas, poor country,

speaker: ROSS
text: Almost afraid to know itself. It cannot

speaker: ROSS
text: Be called our mother, but our grave, where nothing

speaker: ROSS
text: But who knows nothing is once seen to smile;

speaker: ROSS
text: Where sighs and groans and shrieks that rent the air

speaker: ROSS
text: Are made, not marked; where violent sorrow seems

speaker: ROSS
text: A modern ecstasy. The dead man’s knell

speaker: ROSS
text: Is there scarce asked for who, and good men’s lives

speaker: ROSS
text: Expire before the flowers in their caps,

speaker: ROSS
text: Dying or ere they sicken.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: O relation too nice and yet too true!

speaker: MALCOLM
text: What’s the newest grief?

speaker: ROSS
text: That of an hour’s age doth hiss the speaker.

speaker: ROSS
text: Each minute teems a new one.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: How does my wife?

speaker: ROSS
text: Why, well.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And all my children?

speaker: ROSS
text: Well too.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: The tyrant has not battered at their peace?

speaker: ROSS
text: No, they were well at peace when I did leave ’em.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Be not a niggard of your speech. How goes ’t?

speaker: ROSS
text: When I came hither to transport the tidings

speaker: ROSS
text: Which I have heavily borne, there ran a rumor

speaker: ROSS
text: Of many worthy fellows that were out;

speaker: ROSS
text: Which was to my belief witnessed the rather

speaker: ROSS
text: For that I saw the tyrant’s power afoot.

speaker: ROSS
text: Now is the time of help. Your eye in Scotland

speaker: ROSS
text: Would create soldiers, make our women fight

speaker: ROSS
text: To doff their dire distresses.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Be ’t their comfort

speaker: MALCOLM
text: We are coming thither. Gracious England hath

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Lent us good Siward and ten thousand men;

speaker: MALCOLM
text: An older and a better soldier none

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That Christendom gives out.

speaker: ROSS
text: Would I could answer

speaker: ROSS
text: This comfort with the like. But I have words

speaker: ROSS
text: That would be howled out in the desert air,

speaker: ROSS
text: Where hearing should not latch them.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: What concern they—

speaker: MACDUFF
text: The general cause, or is it a fee-grief

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Due to some single breast?

speaker: ROSS
text: No mind that’s honest

speaker: ROSS
text: But in it shares some woe, though the main part

speaker: ROSS
text: Pertains to you alone.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: If it be mine,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Keep it not from me. Quickly let me have it.

speaker: ROSS
text: Let not your ears despise my tongue forever,

speaker: ROSS
text: Which shall possess them with the heaviest sound

speaker: ROSS
text: That ever yet they heard.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Hum! I guess at it.

speaker: ROSS
text: Your castle is surprised, your wife and babes

speaker: ROSS
text: Savagely slaughtered. To relate the manner

speaker: ROSS
text: Were on the quarry of these murdered deer

speaker: ROSS
text: To add the death of you.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Merciful heaven!—

speaker: MALCOLM
text: What, man, ne’er pull your hat upon your brows.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Give sorrow words. The grief that does not speak

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Whispers the o’erfraught heart and bids it break.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: My children too?

speaker: ROSS
text: Wife, children, servants, all that could be found.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And I must be from thence? My wife killed too?

speaker: ROSS
text: I have said.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Be comforted.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Let’s make us med’cines of our great revenge

speaker: MALCOLM
text: To cure this deadly grief.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: He has no children. All my pretty ones?

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Did you say “all”? O hell-kite! All?

speaker: MACDUFF
text: What, all my pretty chickens and their dam

speaker: MACDUFF
text: At one fell swoop?

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Dispute it like a man.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I shall do so,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: But I must also feel it as a man.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I cannot but remember such things were

speaker: MACDUFF
text: That were most precious to me. Did heaven look on

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And would not take their part? Sinful Macduff,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: They were all struck for thee! Naught that I am,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Not for their own demerits, but for mine,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Fell slaughter on their souls. Heaven rest them now.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Be this the whetstone of your sword. Let grief

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Convert to anger. Blunt not the heart; enrage it.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: O, I could play the woman with mine eyes

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And braggart with my tongue! But, gentle heavens,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Cut short all intermission! Front to front

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Bring thou this fiend of Scotland and myself.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Within my sword’s length set him. If he ’scape,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Heaven forgive him too.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: This tune goes manly.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Come, go we to the King. Our power is ready;

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Our lack is nothing but our leave. Macbeth

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Is ripe for shaking, and the powers above

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Put on their instruments. Receive what cheer you may.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: The night is long that never finds the day.
